formData.subject: AFTER ACTION REPORT - EXERCISE STEEL KNIGHT 26.1

paragraphs.text: This memorandum documents the key observations and lessons learned from Exercise Steel Knight 26.1, conducted 6-18 January 2026 at Marine Corps Air Ground Combat Center, Twentynine Palms, CA.

Exercise Steel Knight 26.1 was a Division-level combined arms exercise involving approximately 8,500 Marines and Sailors. The exercise validated the Division's ability to conduct large-scale combat operations in a contested environment per reference (a).
Communications connectivity between regimental combat teams was maintained at 94% uptime, exceeding the 90% threshold established in reference (b).
Logistics sustainment operations demonstrated improved efficiency with a 15% reduction in turnaround time for Class V resupply compared to Steel Knight 25.2.
Integration of unmanned aerial systems (UAS) for reconnaissance significantly enhanced situational awareness at the battalion level.
The following areas require additional training emphasis prior to the next scheduled exercise:
Night operations coordination between ground and aviation elements needs improvement. Three instances of delayed close air support requests were identified during nighttime phases.
Electronic warfare defensive measures require enhanced training. Simulated enemy jamming operations degraded communications for periods exceeding acceptable thresholds during two phases.
Subordinate commands shall incorporate identified lessons learned into their unit training plans not later than 1 March 2026. The G-3 will track implementation through quarterly training reviews.
